title: [Whimsicott] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Whimsicott] is a stat sheet that doesn't matter and an ability that does everything. [prankster] gives every [support] move +1 priority, which means [tailwind], [encore], and [leech-seed] all go before anything short of Choice-Scarf priority. Base 116 Speed is a nice bonus but almost never used — [Whimsicott]'s job is to flip momentum the instant it enters.

## Strengths
The [prankster] package is the entire kit. Turn-one [tailwind] locks the team's Speed advantage for four turns, which alone carries many games. [encore] punishes any setup attempt, any [protect], or any non-attacking click — a single free [encore] against a [Dragon] Dance opponent swings the whole match. [leech-seed] off [prankster] is guaranteed against anything slower, and the [Grass]-[Fairy] type combination gives [Whimsicott] free switch-ins on [Water], [Fighting], [Dragon], and [Dark] STAB, which is where most leads live anyway.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ice], [Poison], [Flying], [Steel], and [Fire] all hit for 2x, and [Whimsicott]'s 60 HP base dies to almost anything not resisted. [Flying] and [Ice] leads outrun and kill it before [prankster] matters — against those matchups it has one turn to deliver [tailwind] and get out. [Dark]-types are the hard [counter] because [prankster] status moves are blanked against them entirely. [moonblast] off 77 SpA is chip damage, not real pressure, so any bulky [Steel] safely walls the set.

## Recommended Moveset
[tailwind] is the identity move — clicking it turn one on the [lead] is why [Whimsicott] exists. [encore] is the second mandatory slot since it punishes setup and recovery clicks for free and often produces a free turn for the [rest] of the team. [leech-seed] is the third slot because it forces switches and generates chip damage on whatever is stuck in front of [Whimsicott]. [moonblast] is the final slot because [Whimsicott] needs at least one offensive option — [substitute] swaps in if the team is built around [prankster]-only utility rather than pressure.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default — it guarantees the turn-one [tailwind] regardless of what the opponent leads with. [mental-herb] answers bait [taunt] leads and preserves the whole [support] plan. [sitrus-berry] fits a [leech-seed] stall variant. The ability has to be [prankster] — [chlorophyll] only matters in a hard-sun team, and [infiltrator] is nowhere near as impactful as priority status. Tera [Steel] is the top Tera pick because it flips [Ice], [Flying], [Poison], and [Fire] all at once; Tera [Fairy] is the alternative when [prankster]'s [Dark]-type vulnerability needs hiding.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Whimsicott] needs slow, high-damage attackers that can cash in on [tailwind] — [trick-room]-adjacent bruisers actually don't want [tailwind], so pair it with fast setup sweepers or physical wallbreakers instead. A [Dark]-type answer ([Fighting] or [Fairy] hitter) is mandatory. Add a [Steel] tank to [absorb] [Ice] and [Flying] pressure, and a hazard-removing pivot so [Whimsicott]'s [focus-sash] doesn't die to [stealth-rock] on switch-in.

## Play Patterns
Lead [Whimsicott] 90 percent of the time. Turn one [tailwind], turn two either [encore] or [leech-seed] depending on what the opponent clicked. If a [Dark]-type leads instead, switch out immediately — [Whimsicott] has nothing to do against them. Once all [prankster] utility is spent, chip out with [moonblast] to guarantee [focus-sash] contribution. Tera only when a [Flying] or [Ice] hit would KO an otherwise-safe [Whimsicott] — otherwise leave Tera for a teammate that will cash it in harder.
